14c7e598-42ac-4fdc-9eca-614176b7a456.name: Splashtop Remote Screen Sharing
14c7e598-42ac-4fdc-9eca-614176b7a456.description: Splashtop is a leading screen share technology that provides a faster and smoother experience than the default VNC. The streamer can be rolled out to all your connected devices, both Windows and Mac, either automatically or on demand.

cc952f49-05f1-4a21-942b-31e014030c70.name: Reboot Required Monitor [WIN]
cc952f49-05f1-4a21-942b-31e014030c70.description: PowerShell (2.0+) script to raise an alert if the system is pending a reboot from Microsoft updates, Configuration Manager Client SDK, Pending Computer Rename, Domain Join, Pending File Rename Operations or Internal Reboot Flag. For Windows 2008+ the function will query the CBS registry key as another factor in determining pending reboot state

variables.name: ChkInternalRebootFlag
variables.description: Internal "reboot.flag" file sometimes created during patch management operations

variables.name: ChkPendComputerRename
variables.description: Detects either a computer rename or domain join operation (Windows 2003+)

variables.name: ChkCCMClientSDK
variables.description: SCCM 2012 Clients only

variables.name: ChkWindowsUpdate
variables.description: Windows Update / Auto Update (Windows 2003+)

variables.name: ChkPendFileRename
variables.description: PendingFileRenameOperations (Windows 2003+). Some Anti-Virus leverage this key for def/dat removal, giving a false positive PendingReboot

variables.name: ChkCBServicing
variables.description: Component Based Servicing (Windows 2008+)

ca9f85c2-6896-460d-8daa-effa7ec48976.name: Ping Monitor v2 [WIN]
ca9f85c2-6896-460d-8daa-effa7ec48976.description:
Monitor to ping a specified host/ip address 10 times in a row with a 5 second timeout and returns an alert status if no  responses are received. 

Input: Host (String) - Host/IP Address to check for.  
Input: Name (String)  - Name of monitor (user defined).

variables.name: Host
variables.description: Single address to ping (i.e. 192.168.1.1 or www.google.co.uk)

variables.name: Result
variables.description: Alert message to display

variables.name: Name
variables.description: Name - enter a short description of the purpose of this ping monitor

1788bba1-6c54-495a-af2d-6167c85c11d2.name: Bandwidth Utilization Monitor [WIN]
1788bba1-6c54-495a-af2d-6167c85c11d2.description: Monitors the average bandwidth usage (megabits/s) of a Windows Endpoint over 30 seconds and alerts if it crosses a predefined threshold.

variables.name: MbpsMax
variables.description: Threshold (megabits/s over 30 seconds) past which a usage alert will be raised.

d79b23cb-713c-4252-98c3-3b6aab4a3264.name: Device Uptime (Percentage) Monitor [WIN]
d79b23cb-713c-4252-98c3-3b6aab4a3264.description: Calculates the percentage of time across the last 30 days that the device in question has been online for. Requires PowerShell.

variables.name: varUDField
variables.description: Configure which UDF to place the device's uptime percentage into.

e73dc445-a2a3-494a-899e-90b98469ffac.name: Autotask
e73dc445-a2a3-494a-899e-90b98469ffac.description: This integration with Autotask PSA can sync your devices , automatically generate tickets and much more!

e86ca69c-2475-4124-84be-dd453dce6da2.name: Mobile Device Management
e86ca69c-2475-4124-84be-dd453dce6da2.description: Add support for iOS and Android devices. Purchase this extension to enable Mobile Device Management  in your account. Before you can enrol any Apple iOS devices you will need to upload your Apple Push Notification Certificate. Instructions to do this can be found in My Account - Settings once the extension has been downloaded. You can then send enrolment emails to your devices from the 'Add Device' link within every Profile. To remove this extension simply delete it from the Extensions category in your Component Library.

32bd4fe2-7d86-43a3-8f78-9c3a2250915e.name: Datto Integration
32bd4fe2-7d86-43a3-8f78-9c3a2250915e.description: Users can enable Datto Integration within their accounts with this extension. To disable the feature, simply delete this component from your Component Library.

7e16fde4-89b9-4fa8-bda9-761bac6ba52a.name: Mirror Driver for uVNC & Splashtop [WIN]
7e16fde4-89b9-4fa8-bda9-761bac6ba52a.description: Install Mirror Driver for uVNC and Splashtop on Windows 10, 8.1, 8, 7, Vista and Server 2016, 2012 R2, 2012, 2008 R2, 2008. Installation will abort if an incompatible operating system or previous driver installation is found.

16eeb25a-57fb-4037-9f17-38efc40d84ed.name: Microsoft Office 16 Build & Licence Detector
16eeb25a-57fb-4037-9f17-38efc40d84ed.description: Apply this Component Monitor to your Windows devices to run a version check and place the data discovered in a user-defined field. You can use this for reporting.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Number between 1-30 to determine which UDF the data ought to be placed into.

ade0a83e-cdee-4bfc-82b7-f7ccc20b54e7.name: Website Checker [WIN]
ade0a83e-cdee-4bfc-82b7-f7ccc20b54e7.description: Checks the HTML on a page on a given URI for a string, upon which a pass/failure condition can be calculated. Can either be used to check connectivity or to alert on the presence of a certain string on a page. Requires PowerShell 3+.

variables.name: usrString
variables.description: String to search for upon which a success/failure condition will be calculated.

variables.name: usrURI
variables.description: URI to check for a string, upon which a pass/failure condition can be ascertained.

ac8dd5c8-a406-4164-afec-5f7495756997.name: Port Checker [WIN]
ac8dd5c8-a406-4164-afec-5f7495756997.description: This Monitor Component will check whether a specified endpoint address has a specified TCP port reachable from the device that the monitor is applied to. An alert will be triggered when the port is unreachable from that device or if the specfied host address is not reachable. NOTE: This monitor should only be set on devices that have had the 'Portqry Installer' component successfully deployed.

variables.name: address
variables.description: FQDN (web address) or IP address

variables.name: port
variables.description: TCP Port number

variables.name: CSMON_ResultMessage
variables.description: Monitor output message

d7cd0cb9-d503-4b6b-a809-ca6099b3d522.name: USB Drive Detection Monitor [WIN]
d7cd0cb9-d503-4b6b-a809-ca6099b3d522.description: Monitor to check and alert on the presence of a USB drive. Alert message will include device model and disk capacity in MB.

faf6cb2d-ff9f-40bd-acc8-1e1e03a4749b.name: Autoruns [WIN]
faf6cb2d-ff9f-40bd-acc8-1e1e03a4749b.description: Autoruns is an app that shows you what apps are configured to run during your system bootup or login. It also shows you the entries in the order Windows processes them. These apps include ones in your Startup folder, Run, RunOnce, and other Registry keys

3ddc5c10-c06d-4abb-8d6e-52ca17b9eedd.name: Portqry Installer [WIN]
3ddc5c10-c06d-4abb-8d6e-52ca17b9eedd.description: Deploy Microsoft's 'Portqry.exe' program. Can be used in association with the Port Checker Monitor, also available in the ComStore

65c31a48-201f-43fe-9459-df2e15f79f7d.name: Process Monitor [WIN]
65c31a48-201f-43fe-9459-df2e15f79f7d.description: Process Monitor is an advanced monitoring tool for Windows that shows real-time file system, Registry and process/thread activity. It combines the features of two legacy Sysinternals utilities, Filemon and Regmon, and adds an extensive list of enhancements including rich and non-destructive filtering, comprehensive event properties such session IDs and user names, reliable process information, full thread stacks with integrated symbol support for each operation, simultaneous logging to a file, and much more. Its uniquely powerful features will make Process Monitor a core utility in your system troubleshooting and malware hunting toolkit.

597b9e44-f524-4af6-bffe-a42866090272.name: Active Directory Audit (SPLA)
597b9e44-f524-4af6-bffe-a42866090272.description: Active Directory Audit script for AEM. This script counts the number of Active users on a Windows Domain Controller that have logged in in the last 30 days. It will output to STDOUT and store this number in the user-defined fields.

variables.name: Customfield2
variables.description: Changed from last Audit

variables.name: Customfield1
variables.description: Total active users found

cebe5160-704c-45c5-a2dd-237666f160f8.name: BitDefender Antivirus 2016 Monitor [WIN]
cebe5160-704c-45c5-a2dd-237666f160f8.description:
This component monitor will check and alert for: 

BitDefender Antivirus service not running, 
Out of Date Signatures. 

Compatible with:

Win 7 (32/64),
Win 8 (32/64),
Win 8.1 (32/64),
Win 10 (32/64)

variables.name: uptodate
variables.description: Period (in days) that monitor will check back over i.e. If BitDefender Antivirus has not been updated within the last x days, an alert will be triggered (Default setting is 7 days)

131d7e02-64f5-4fa8-a506-119eebb9469d.name: Change user password [WIN]
131d7e02-64f5-4fa8-a506-119eebb9469d.description: Changes the password for a user specified at execution time. Run on a Domain Controller will change the password for a domain user. Run on a member workstation, stand-alone workstation or stand-alone server will change the password for a local user.

variables.name: cs_username
variables.description: User name to have password changed

variables.name: cs_password
variables.description: New password

0535f5aa-e322-4240-9e4a-6cdd7d2bbd83.name: Create New Administrative User [WIN]
0535f5aa-e322-4240-9e4a-6cdd7d2bbd83.description: Creates a new user and makes them a member of the local administrators group (For Windows)

variables.name: cs_password
variables.description: Password to add

variables.name: cs_username
variables.description: Username to add

variables.name: usrNeverExpire
variables.description: Set to true to disable password expiration.

9a5a880d-fd63-453e-a488-746236a764fc.name: Defraggler Defrag [WIN]
9a5a880d-fd63-453e-a488-746236a764fc.description: Defrag selected drive using standalone version of Defraggler which does not require installation to function

variables.name: drive
variables.description: Drive letter to defrag in the format of C:

89ea404f-26ba-4a97-ad0f-69589c6338b1.name: Detect Backup Product [WIN]
89ea404f-26ba-4a97-ad0f-69589c6338b1.description: Use this script to identify what Backup suite is installed on a Windows device and populate the data found into a User-Defined Field.

variables.name: Custom
variables.description: Enter the custom field you wish to use in the following format: Custom6

5911b1b7-f5df-438a-91fc-13828fbd63de.name: Detect Windows and Office keys  [WIN]
5911b1b7-f5df-438a-91fc-13828fbd63de.description: This component will detect Windows and Office licence keys used on a device and write them in the STDOUT and User-defined fields 9 and 10 respectively. This component does not support Office 365 or Office 2016 onwards.

eeacb1b0-bf72-4eff-aa6f-fe94a08238f1.name: Disable Windows 10 Suggestions and Recommendations [WIN]
eeacb1b0-bf72-4eff-aa6f-fe94a08238f1.description: Use this script to disable various suggestions and recommendations (adverts) from Windows 10. Can be used to disable automatic re-opening of apps when a system reboots on Windows 10 build 1709 and higher.

variables.name: disableAutoReopen
variables.description: Disable Windows 10 automatically re-opening apps on reboot that were open when the Endpoint shut down.

3ee1f650-db7c-4daa-8c12-2a1ac459b15b.name: Email Server IP Blocklist Check [WIN]
3ee1f650-db7c-4daa-8c12-2a1ac459b15b.description: Checks regularly against ~70 Email spam blocklist servers and reports back if the IP of the endpoint is listed as belonging to an email-spam operation.

53e09f98-d545-40c4-947f-e4723924b7fe.name: Naverisk Uninstaller [WIN]
53e09f98-d545-40c4-947f-e4723924b7fe.description: Use this Component to remove all traces of the Naverisk NAS Agent from a Windows device.

2042c770-b4f3-47c8-8f26-51c2fc60c1a8.name: Network Speed Test [WIN]
2042c770-b4f3-47c8-8f26-51c2fc60c1a8.description: This component will check the current Ping, Upload and Download speeds by checking the connection to a near server. Results are posted in StdOut; information can also be furnished into a User-Defined Field.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Set whether to output to a User-Defined Field.

0ebe3d02-a45c-4f6c-8e26-92df9cf89292.name: Server Role Detection [WIN]
0ebe3d02-a45c-4f6c-8e26-92df9cf89292.description:
This component get  a comprehensive list of installed service roles and writes these to a User-Defined field in the following format, delimited by colon. Allows the creation of custom filters by role.

ie :SQL:IIS:HyperV:File:Print:

Thanks to Alex Bidhendy for this script

d76ad738-29a0-4922-9678-a691b9758d37.name: Wake-On-Lan All Discovered Devices [WIN]
d76ad738-29a0-4922-9678-a691b9758d37.description: This component looks at the Discovered devices list that's available on an Agent that's running the Network Node with Scanning  function. It will select all Windows device, and sends the MAC address of these devices the Wake-On-Lan Command.

083eafde-24f2-4ce0-9f64-9f3ed37e0b33.name: Domain Name SSL Certificate Expiry Check [WIN]
083eafde-24f2-4ce0-9f64-9f3ed37e0b33.description: PowerShell Script to check the expiration date of a web Security Certificate. Can be configured to alert if the certificate's remaining validity period (in days) is lower than a pre-configured threshold.

variables.name: ExpiryLimit
variables.description: Time in days before certificate expires. If data returned is lower than this number an alert will sound.

variables.name: URL
variables.description: Domain to check. Do not use quote marks or slashes; only the DNS-level domain is expected.

6017cde9-3763-48cf-81f1-7c678e568d00.name: 7-Zip [WIN]
6017cde9-3763-48cf-81f1-7c678e568d00.description: 7-Zip is a file archiver for Windows. The main feature is the 7z format, which delivers a high compression ratio.

variables.name: varAction
variables.description: Which action to take

variables.name: usr7zFileAssoc
variables.description: Associate ZIP, 7Z, RAR, CPT files with 7-Zip? (Apply this variable at Site-/Global-level for Software Management)

5a037fad-d346-4691-bf0b-c6f530f37f4e.name: Adobe Flash Player [WIN]
5a037fad-d346-4691-bf0b-c6f530f37f4e.description: This Component has been deprecated and can no longer be used.

623e9595-4606-4a6f-9856-3ea694a53fcf.name: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC [WIN]
623e9595-4606-4a6f-9856-3ea694a53fcf.description: This component will update or install Adobe Acrobat Reader DC to the latest version using the MUI or localised version where applicable. This Component is only compatible with Acrobat products on the DC track; iterative releases named after years are not supported.

f142ed9a-f959-4175-a931-1ef42e3987c6.name: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC [MAC]
f142ed9a-f959-4175-a931-1ef42e3987c6.description: Adobe Reader software is the free trusted standard for reliably viewing, printing, and annotating PDF documents. It's the only PDF file viewer that can open and interact with all types of PDF content, including forms and multimedia.

aef2e3de-cc28-41d8-8fa5-3a7e8bf9a31b.name: Google Chrome [WIN]
aef2e3de-cc28-41d8-8fa5-3a7e8bf9a31b.description: Installs or updates Google Chrome to the latest version. Automatically determines the native Chrome build for the system for fresh installations; for updates, sticks with previous build type despite nativity. This can be overridden by changing usrAction to 'Upgrade'. Supports Windows x86, x86-64, and Arm64.

variables.name: usrUninstall
variables.description: Completely remove all System- and User-level installations of Chrome and do not install a new version.

variables.name: usrAction
variables.description: Which action for the Component to take. Software Management runs will always use 'Install'.

c276822f-cec0-4ec8-bc9a-292d5e0f35d3.name: Java Runtime Environment 8 [WIN]
c276822f-cec0-4ec8-bc9a-292d5e0f35d3.description: Downloads and installs the latest version of Java Runtime Environment version 8 for Windows. Installs 64- and 32-bit builds on 64-bit devices and just 32-bit on 32-bit devices. Set Site or Account variable usrJavaClean to value 'true' to remove old Java versions.

704ca127-34be-4866-9149-9f74124ad5d2.name: Create "New Support Ticket" Shortcut v2 [WIN]
704ca127-34be-4866-9149-9f74124ad5d2.description: For all users of the Endpoint, this script will create a shortcut on the Desktop that can be used to create a new Support ticket. This will only function if the Site in which the Devices appear is mapped to an Autotask PSA Company with an active Agent Policy enabling the System Tray ticketing options.

variables.name: usrPublic
variables.description: Instead of creating individual shortcuts for each user, create one single shortcut in the Public/All Users directory. Note that this shortcut will disappear for all users if one user deletes it.

76edfeaf-d828-4a52-9617-5d955a9cb493.name: Security Audit [WIN]
76edfeaf-d828-4a52-9617-5d955a9cb493.description: Use this Component to help get your Windows devices into a more secure state by auditing various settings known to present security issues when misconfigured. Most effective when used alongside the BitLocker Audit and Windows Update Diagnostic Components. Works alongside the Security Audit monitoring Policy available as a Best-Practice Policy.

e8d1e871-e728-4bc3-9308-8e7a8218cb93.name: Reboot Device [WIN]
e8d1e871-e728-4bc3-9308-8e7a8218cb93.description: Reboot device forcibly with variable timeout

variables.name: timeout
variables.description: Set the time-out period before shutdown to X seconds

9d05ab37-3522-47ff-bbe8-6324ccb80e1c.name: Office Default APP uninstall
9d05ab37-3522-47ff-bbe8-6324ccb80e1c.description: This will uninstall default office app from windows 10

3549766b-c5ff-422d-add4-c2e0e8176d72.name: Windows 10 Upgrade - Professional x86/x64 [WIN]
3549766b-c5ff-422d-add4-c2e0e8176d72.description: This component can be used to upgrade a device running Windows 7 SP1-10 to a recent build of Windows 10. This component only supports Professional versions of Windows. Please allow several hours for Endpoints to complete the download and installation process. More information: http://dat.to/w10uc1

variables.name: usrShowOOBE
variables.description: Set to "true" to display the Windows 10 out-of-box-experience questions to the user or "false" to bypass and use default installation settings.

variables.name: usrImagePath
variables.defaultVal: Supply URI if usrLanguage is unset
variables.description: Unset usrLanguage to use this. URI of Win10 ISO to download. This can be changed to point to an SMB share.

variables.name: usrBuild
variables.description: Select which build of Windows 10 to install if you are using Datto's Windows ISO. Ignore if you are using your own.

variables.name: usrLanguage
variables.description: Datto RMM provide ISOs for UK and US English Win10. Select "Use usrImagePath" to provide your own ISO URI.

9c722224-b482-40fa-b264-8a646e0a49b2.name: Printer Spooler -  Clear and Restart [WIN]
9c722224-b482-40fa-b264-8a646e0a49b2.description: Script that will stop the Printer Spooler service, clear down any troublesome pending print jobs and then restart the service.

abd3df46-0aca-41b2-b176-ff815a1bb589.name: Datto Networking
abd3df46-0aca-41b2-b176-ff815a1bb589.description: This integration with Datto Networking can sync your Datto devices to RMM. To disable the feature, simply delete this component from your Component Library.

78861c45-0e59-4ab0-a3dd-5a9369d4dc96.name: Block Pastebin Access in HOSTS [WIN]
78861c45-0e59-4ab0-a3dd-5a9369d4dc96.description: Block access via HOSTS to popular paste-bins at Pastebin.com, Hashb.in, Anonpaste.org, 0bin.net, ControlC.com/Tny.cz, Paste.ee, Privatebin.net, Hastebin.com, Termbin.com, PasteFS.com, DPaste.com and Dumpz.org. These sites carry little business purpose and are known to be exploited by malware writers to spread malicious code. Blocking these sites is considered a security best-practice.

variables.name: usrRevert
variables.description: Remove any existing HOSTS-based restrictions to pastebins blocked by this script. Use with caution.

c02c212e-2121-4c67-bcdf-8e778645fe0a.name: Create System Restore Point [WIN]
c02c212e-2121-4c67-bcdf-8e778645fe0a.description:
Creates a new System Restore point on a device running Windows 7 or newer.
On Windows 8 and above, if a restore point was created within the last 24 hours, a new one will not be created by default.  You can edit a registry setting to change this.
If system restore is disabled, the script will attempt to enable it before creating the restore point.

variables.name: myRestorePointName
variables.description: Enter a name for the restore point you are creating

86363411-e2ed-418f-b565-e4df8b34245c.name: Hyper-V: Create Snapshot/Checkpoint [WIN]
86363411-e2ed-418f-b565-e4df8b34245c.description:
Creates a Hyper-V Snapshot/Checkpoint of the VM name entered. 

Wildcards are allowed, If multiple VMs match it will create a snapshot for each VM.

variables.name: VMName
variables.description: Name of the virtual machine. Wildcards permitted. All VMs matching wildcard will be snapshot.

variables.name: SnapshotName
variables.description: Snapshot name. The snapshot name can be used to document the reason for the snapshot.

4ef81098-3b77-4721-9bbb-dd99204aff9c.name: Monitor Azure MFA Server [WIN]
4ef81098-3b77-4721-9bbb-dd99204aff9c.description:
Monitors if users have MFA enabled on Azure MFA server. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-monitoring-mfa-server-and-office365-mfa-status/

4f15c654-0ee0-414e-b78f-0574f43f9bb3.name: Monitor Breached Passwords [WIN]
4f15c654-0ee0-414e-b78f-0574f43f9bb3.description:
Monitors if the passwords entered have been breached via the 'Have I Been Pwned' API. Passwords will not be sent to external party - a generated hash will. Alerts if the password is known to be breached. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/using-powershell-to-check-pwned-passwords-using-the-haveibeenpwned-api/

variables.name: Passwords
variables.description: Enter multiple passwords to check, comma separated.

e953c730-af59-45f6-b594-7c0ae8c2fa24.name: Monitor External Open Ports [WIN]
e953c730-af59-45f6-b594-7c0ae8c2fa24.description:
Performs an external port scan. Requires setup of port scan API first. See related blog. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-external-port-scanning/

variables.name: PortScanURL
variables.description: Location where you've hosted the scan file.

4a734a5e-cc42-4fed-b929-2d1ba7730a5a.name: Monitor Print Servers and Queues [WIN]
4a734a5e-cc42-4fed-b929-2d1ba7730a5a.description:
Monitors the print server and queues for the current status of both printers and jobs. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-3-hyper-v-state/

26ac5303-0681-4d2d-a9ca-8a6a66f2336f.name: Monitor RDS Encryption Level [WIN]
26ac5303-0681-4d2d-a9ca-8a6a66f2336f.description:
Monitors the Encryption level of the RDS deployment

Supported OS: Server 2012R2 or up. Requires RDS deployment.

Related blog:https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-2-monitor-rds-security-and-licensing-status/

f5117cb5-83ad-4d8f-8e48-9d2f2eb8e9f5.name: Monitor RDS License Mode [WIN]
f5117cb5-83ad-4d8f-8e48-9d2f2eb8e9f5.description:
Monitors the License mode of the RDS server. Please select Per-user or Per-device mode. Requires RDS deployment.

Supported OS: Server 2012R2 and up.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-2-monitor-rds-security-and-licensing-status/

variables.name: LicenseMode
variables.description: What License mode should be monitored

892d0ce3-75bc-40e3-89d9-89fe3a61002a.name: Monitor RDS Network-Level Authentication [WIN]
892d0ce3-75bc-40e3-89d9-89fe3a61002a.description:
Monitors if Network Level Authentication is enabled in a RDS deployment. Requires RDS deployment.

Supported OS: 2012R2 and up.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-2-monitor-rds-security-and-licensing-status/

d5be5ae0-eecf-4592-8696-ebf66dbb9265.name: Bitdefender GravityZone - Deployment [WIN][MAC]
d5be5ae0-eecf-4592-8696-ebf66dbb9265.description:
Use this component to deploy and schedule auto-deployment jobs for the Bitdefender security agent. The Bitdefender agent runs the installation files through Setup Downloader (small-sized file). 

Component installation requires an MSP trial or monthly subscription and a Partner account to access Bitdefender GravityZone, a unified management console for advanced endpoint protection. 

For more information, refer to http://download.bitdefender.com/SMB/DattoRMM/documentation/enUS/Bitdefender_Component_User_Guide.pdf.

c92f147b-2044-41d7-90a1-70a167aafab1.name: Disk Cleanup [WIN]
c92f147b-2044-41d7-90a1-70a167aafab1.description: Disk Cleanup script for Windows. Supports Windows 7 SP1 onward. Clears global and user-specific Temp directories. Optionally clears the Recycle Bin, PreFetch, user-level browser caches, minidumps and the Windows Update cache.

variables.name: usrPreFetch
variables.description: Clear device PreFetch?

variables.name: usrMinidump
variables.description: Clear device Minidump files?

variables.name: usrPackagesFolder
variables.description: Clear out cached RMM Components aside from this one?

variables.name: usrUpdateCache

86fb0890-b9bd-4d50-9585-25ff2dbb1d55.name: Bitdefender GravityZone - Deployment [WIN][MAC][LIN]
86fb0890-b9bd-4d50-9585-25ff2dbb1d55.description:
Use this component to deploy and schedule auto-deployment jobs for the Bitdefender security agent. The Bitdefender agent runs the installation files through Setup Downloader (small-sized file). 

Component installation requires an MSP trial or monthly subscription and a Partner account to access Bitdefender GravityZone, a unified management console for advanced endpoint protection. 

For more information, refer to http://download.bitdefender.com/SMB/DattoRMM/documentation/enUS/Bitdefender_Component_User_Guide.pdf.

Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-monitoring-onedrive-status-for-current-logged-on-user/

For more information, refer to the Bitdefender Component User Guide (https://www.bitdefender.com/business/support/en/77211-117533-datto.html).

18389f4c-1ff5-4d8f-a24d-b82937a619bc.name: Bitdefender GravityZone - Device Management [WIN][MAC][LIN]
18389f4c-1ff5-4d8f-a24d-b82937a619bc.description: Legacy: Use this component to deploy and schedule auto-deployment jobs for the Bitdefender security agent. The Bitdefender agent runs the installation files through Setup Downloader (small-sized file). Component installation requires an MSP trial or monthly subscription and a Partner account to access Bitdefender GravityZone, a unified management console for advanced endpoint protection. This Component is also used to configure response actions for monitoring policies for the Datto Legacy integration (https://www.bitdefender.com/business/support/en/77211-117533-datto.html). Through the available response actions, you can deploy or update the security agent. For more information, refer to the Bitdefender Component User Guide (https://www.bitdefender.com/business/support/en/77211-117533-datto.html).

b0c17d7c-e4b4-461c-86a8-9bbd41af217d.name: Disk Space Monitor [MAC][LIN]
b0c17d7c-e4b4-461c-86a8-9bbd41af217d.description: Use this unified monitor to test all disks connected to an Endpoint and alert if their occupied space crosses a pre-defined threshold. USB devices are omitted for Windows devices; DMGs are omitted for Macs. macOS data disparities with Disk Utility are typically due to local snapshots. Windows support is limited to devices with WMIC; newer devices should use Datto RMM native monitoring instead.

variables.name: usrThreshold
variables.description: Integer representing the percentage of full disk capacity beyond which point an alert will be raised. Do not use the percentage sign.

variables.name: usrDisks
variables.description: Space-separated list of disks to check, or "ALL". Windows disks need colons, eg C: D:; Nix drives look like /dev/sda1 or /dev/disk1s3.

1f0b85ba-40e0-4fa1-8079-b380267cd808.name: Deploy RMM Agent by AD GPO [WIN]

variables.name: RemoveGPO
variables.description: Control whether or not to use the component to remove the GPO and folder/contents; set to True to do this instead of setting up GPO deployment.

variables.name: RecreateCSV
variables.description: Control whether or not to recreate the CSV file required for OU targeting. Set both this variable and UseOUTargeting to True to do this, otherwise leave False

variables.name: ImmediateGPUpdate
variables.description: Control whether or not to run an immediate and silent Group Policy update across all your domain-joined computers once the GPO is imported and linked. True will do this. Set to False to wait for next automatic refresh

variables.name: UseOUTargeting
variables.description: Control targeting of GPO link(s) and/or destination sites. False will create a link at domain root and associate all computers to the DC site. Set to True to target by OU, and/or associate OUs to different sites, based on a pre-populated generated CSV file. First run will generate the file, subsequent run will deploy the Agents accordingly.

60f4fe49-3d77-40de-b299-e1f36e148198.name: CCleaner [MAC]

2192c43a-e1e5-49ff-b3a6-a35dda49033c.name: Malwarebytes Monitor [MAC]
2192c43a-e1e5-49ff-b3a6-a35dda49033c.description:

An alert will be created if the endpoint agent service stops on the endpoint. This monitor does NOT check against NOR alerts on detected threats.

antivirus.json is updated and diagnostic are created.

d9ca1dff-5e75-42c6-8f09-92ae8b0be438.description: This component gets a comprehensive list of installed service roles and writes these to a User-Defined field in the following format, delimited by colon. Allows the creation of custom filters by role, ie :SQL:IIS:HyperV:File:Print:.

4d31b456-bd10-49e2-a8b6-78a5321b44a7.description: Windows 10 onward only. Display a 'toast'-style notification for the logged-in user. Supports custom images. A localised option to reboot now can be displayed. Not tested on Servers.

variables.name: usrHeroImgURL

variables.name: usrMessageContent

e3657152-5a99-450a-af34-cc4ca0766342.name: Threatdown OneView Endpoint Agent Deployment [WIN]
e3657152-5a99-450a-af34-cc4ca0766342.description:
Install or Uninstall Threatdown Endpoint Agent on Windows devices with this component - Be aware there are reports that this installer may reboot devices.

When scheduling a job using this component, select install, uninstall or repair in the 'run_type' Variables drop-down. 

NOTE: The OneView Site's Account Token must be defined at Site- or Account-level as 'MWB_AccountToken' in order to properly deploy the Endpoint Agent and report to the correct Site. Specify an anti-tamper password by defining the MWB_Tamper_password Site variable. For more information on endpoint system requirements, refer to the Threatdown Support Documentation: https://support.malwarebytes.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038522274-System-requirements-for-Malwarebytes-Nebula-

variables.name: MWB_runtype
variables.description: Specifiy if endpoint agent will be installed or uninstalled. Tamper-protection MUST BE OFF for uninstall. Delete Corrupted Endpoint option will fully delete ThreatDown EPA from Device and restart it.

fbc5673d-599e-4d07-bfaa-8ea25cfdfa89.name: Threatdown OneView Endpoint Agent Deployment - Server [WIN]
fbc5673d-599e-4d07-bfaa-8ea25cfdfa89.description:

When scheduling a job using this component, select install, uninstall or repair in the 'run_type' Variables drop-down. 

NOTE: The OneView Site's Account Token must be defined at Site- or Account-level as 'MWB_AccountToken' in order to properly deploy the Endpoint Agent and report to the correct Site. Specify an anti-tamper password by defining the MWB_Tamper_password Site variable. For more information on endpoint system requirements, refer to the Malwarebytes Support Documentation: https://support.malwarebytes.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038522274-System-requirements-for-Malwarebytes-Nebula-

variables.name: MWB_runtype
variables.description: Specifiy if endpoint agent will be installed or uninstalled. If using Tamper protection, define the MWB_tamper_password variable at the Site- or Global-level.

cbc39729-6734-4526-a2cc-f97614359934.name: Domain Controller Monitor v2 [WIN]
cbc39729-6734-4526-a2cc-f97614359934.description: This component runs DCDIAG on your Active Directory Domain Controllers, and will alert if one of the 6 key tests is failed. New 2021 release supports non-English systems.

ba4cb463-2b3f-4af5-8a2f-235d3ecc9b81.name: Unblock upgrade to Windows 11 [WIN]
ba4cb463-2b3f-4af5-8a2f-235d3ecc9b81.description: Removes the block to allow upgrade to Windows 11. This Component does not upgrade the machine to Windows 11, but allows you to install the update when it becomes available.

variables.defaultVal: Unblock and allow manual Upgrade Assistant
variables.description: Whether to permit use of the Upgrade Assistant when unblocking.

e81274ba-3b5c-43f1-a571-97af4c105d99.name: Windows 11 Readiness Check [WIN]
e81274ba-3b5c-43f1-a571-97af4c105d99.description: Uses Microsoft's official readiness check script to check if the machine is capable of running Windows 11. Outputs the results both to output and a UDF if selected. Uses https://aka.ms/HWReadinessScript.

variables.name: UDFOutput
variables.description: Enable to store output in a UDF

4130c746-d206-4718-adb9-e6ed781a4e6f.name: Block Upgrade to Windows 11 [WIN]
4130c746-d206-4718-adb9-e6ed781a4e6f.description: Blocks the upgrade to Windows 11 by instructing Windows to stay at the last available version of Windows 10; the default is 21H2. PLEASE NOTE: This will override any existing feature update deferrals set on the Endpoint.

28497336-4430-4eff-b55c-8ff35c651c2d.name: BitLocker & TPM Audit [WIN]
28497336-4430-4eff-b55c-8ff35c651c2d.description: Appraises the presence and specification version of a trusted platform module (TPM) on the endpoint's motherboard; checks each disk (or just the system drive) and gives its BitLocker status, encryption type and recovery key. Output can be written to a UDF.

variables.name: usrDisks
variables.description: Check BitLocker status for all fixed disks, or just the system drive?

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

variables.name: usrAlert
variables.description: Prefix articles potentially requiring attention with "Notice" to trigger output text warnings (orange bar).

variables.name: usrGetRecovery
variables.description: For each encrypted disk, return recovery keys?

8d0b99bd-8b4d-4a00-8c9c-e9b17b91e4b0.name: Change Hostname [WIN]
8d0b99bd-8b4d-4a00-8c9c-e9b17b91e4b0.description: Change the hostname of a Windows device (not supported for domain-joined devices). Changes do not take effect until after a reboot.

variables.name: usrNewName
variables.description: New hostname for the device

variables.name: usrReboot
variables.description: Reboot the system afterwards to forcibly initiate hostname changeover?

01aab4e3-462c-4808-b119-dddca6aed81f.name: DattoTrack [WIN]
01aab4e3-462c-4808-b119-dddca6aed81f.description: Designed to be run on devices that have been stolen in order to attempt to locate them. It puts the Agent into 'Stealth Mode' by performing the following actions: Removing the Agent System Tray icon, removing the Agent from Add/Remove Programs, making all Agent application folders protected operating system files (i.e. SuperHidden), removing Agent shortcuts from the start menu, reporting back external IP Address and performing a TRACEROUTE command to try and get ISP information. The Agent will continue to function in the background as usual but not be easily visible to the thief. The process is irreversible so USE WITH CAUTION!

c41861fe-75ce-47bc-8283-546606bcff3b.name: Export Scheduled Tasks List to StdOut [WIN]
c41861fe-75ce-47bc-8283-546606bcff3b.description: Exports a list of active Scheduled Tasks to StdOut.

66383b59-4111-4981-8c8d-36f83feb93d3.name: File Existence/Change Monitor [WIN]
66383b59-4111-4981-8c8d-36f83feb93d3.description: Monitors a user-defined file path and alerts if the file was last changed more than 24 hours ago, or if the file is absent.

variables.name: FileToBeFound
variables.description: Absolute path to folder/file to be monitored. Do not use quote marks.

0a1220b4-8b62-4a33-9dc6-af82bff316ed.name: Get OU Membership from AD [WIN]
0a1220b4-8b62-4a33-9dc6-af82bff316ed.description:
Gets the OU membership of the local computer from Active Directory and writes the output to User-defined Field 1.
To remove 'OU=' from output, copy this component and edit it to use '$OUMainName' instead of '$OUName'.

2496c7ff-e122-4f27-a34f-344b105fd682.name: Monitor Bluescreens [WIN]
2496c7ff-e122-4f27-a34f-344b105fd682.description:
Monitors Bluescreens (bugchecks, BSODs) via BSODViewer. The 'Deploy BlueScreenView' Component must be run first.
Required OS: Server 2008 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-monitoring-bsods-without-event-viewer/

variables.name: BlueScreenViewPath
variables.description: Location to BlueScreenView.exe. Run the Deploy BlueScreenView Component to place BSV into this location.

7dd928fc-1aa4-4762-94ed-0d35d1ece763.name: Monitor Device Movement [WIN]
7dd928fc-1aa4-4762-94ed-0d35d1ece763.description:
This component checks external IP address against profile variables to detect movement of the device.  

This can be used to generate a ticket to ensure assets are ultimately billed to the correct Account within Autotask.
Requirement: Create two profile variables - EXTIP and EXTIPALT - and define the primary and if existing, alternate external ip address (ie. some sites have redundant connections).

variables.name: EXTIPALT
variables.description: Enter an alternate external IP address for this Profile (Account). ***This will override the profile variable of EXTIPALT.

variables.name: EXTIP
variables.description: Enter the external IP address for this Profile (Account).***This will override the profile variable of EXTIP.

variables.name: Website2
variables.description: Alternate website used to attempt to check external IP address

variables.name: Website1
variables.description: First website used to attempt to check external IP address

variables.name: Result
variables.description: This will be the alert based on no matching IP found when evaluating against the required profile variables defined for the external IP and alternate external IP.

f5faf69b-eec8-475f-a5b2-64111a93f61d.name: Monitor External Open Ports v2 [WIN]
f5faf69b-eec8-475f-a5b2-64111a93f61d.description:
Performs an external port scan. See related blog. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-external-port-scanning-part-2/

variables.name: Ports
variables.description: Ports you want to scan, comma separated

e5ec6482-683d-4ea4-81a7-ae847e123465.name: Monitor if Application is not installed [WIN]
e5ec6482-683d-4ea4-81a7-ae847e123465.description: Monitors if an application is not installed so that the installer Component can be run on alerting machines. Uses the software name as identified in the Apps list in the OS or the Software Audit within Datto RMM.

variables.name: VersionCheck
variables.description: Should we check if the version number is an exact match?

variables.name: VersionNumber
variables.description: Software Version to check. Can be blank if not required.

variables.name: Application
variables.description: The application name to look for, as identified in the Software Audit. Only one item will be returned if multiple items are found.

0f7fc489-1323-4372-8ae1-21ef77a6b638.name: Monitor IIS SSL Certificates [WIN]
0f7fc489-1323-4372-8ae1-21ef77a6b638.description:
Monitors the SSL certificates on IIS via a date set by you. When IIS is not installed, the Component will reply that the service is healthy.

Supported OS: Server 2012R2 and higher.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-3-monitoring-ssl-certificates-on-iis/

variables.name: CertState
variables.description: The state of the Certificate Health

variables.name: Days

039c62a3-c673-4e91-9830-9df4122b434f.name: Process Explorer [WIN]
039c62a3-c673-4e91-9830-9df4122b434f.description: Process Explorer is a lightweight and portable advanced process management utility that picks up where Task Manager leaves off. The app will show you detailed information about a process including its icon, command-line, full image path, memory statistics, user account and security attributes.

04d48edf-f3ea-4847-b646-5c70583658a5.name: Send Message [WIN]
04d48edf-f3ea-4847-b646-5c70583658a5.description: Sends a message in a VBS popup. Must be run as a scheduled job as the logged-in user. Make sure to check "Execute when user is logged in".

variables.description: Notification Title

c72dbb84-6af4-4b29-b578-71592a001204.name: Uninstall Windows Update by KB Number for Windows 7 & 8 [WIN]
c72dbb84-6af4-4b29-b578-71592a001204.description: This component will uninstall Windows Updates and Security Patches for Windows 7 and Windows 8 ONLY. Windows 10 onward cannot reliably uninstall updates. Please note that uninstalling Windows update can seriously harm a system. Thanks to Brandon Phipps of the Datto Community

variables.name: KB
variables.description: Enter KB Update you would like to remove.

094f5326-c374-4650-b506-b9cbc38b3aed.name: Uninstall Software Installed via MSI (DEPRECATED) [WIN]
094f5326-c374-4650-b506-b9cbc38b3aed.description: Deprecated in favour of "Uninstall MSI Software" Component

variables.name: NameLike
variables.description: Software name to search for using the PowerShell -like operator. Supports wildcards (*) but will function as 'equals' if none are included. Use wildcards either side of string to function as 'contains'. Must be at least 3 alphanumeric characters when 'Uninstall' is set to 'True'

variables.name: Uninstall
variables.description: True = Search and uninstall matches / False = Search and output matches but do not uninstall

d99cf8ba-8e3a-4814-8d46-21467514051c.name: Log4Shell Enumeration, Mitigation and Attack Detection Tool [WIN]
d99cf8ba-8e3a-4814-8d46-21467514051c.description: Checks for, and attempts to mitigate against, vulnerabilities found in unsafe versions of Log4j (CVE-2021-44228). Can be configured to write to a UDF; filter on L4JBAD or L4JGOOD as behoves.

variables.name: usrMitigate
variables.description: Enable LOG4J_FORMAT_MSG_NO_LOOKUPS via environment variable, which mitigates the threat on insecure versions of Log4j.

variables.name: usrUpdateDefs
variables.description: Attempt to update the YARA ruleset used to detect insecure code/exploitations? A fallback will be used if declined or if attempts fail.

variables.name: usrScanScope
variables.description: Scope of file scan. Looks for JAR files to detect insecure log4j traces and LOG/TXT files to detect exploitation attempts.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Decide which UDF to write a summary to (if any).

941b799a-f8a8-4d00-9bcd-68d61d2ef33f.name: Monitor Conditional Disk space [WIN]
941b799a-f8a8-4d00-9bcd-68d61d2ef33f.description:
Monitors disk space based on conditionals instead of static values.
Allows you to exclude disks from monitoring via UDF.

Supported OS: Server 2012 and higher
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-conditional-disk-space-monitoring/

variables.name: SmallDiskAbsolute
variables.description: What absolute value to monitor for small disks

variables.name: LargeDiskThreshold
variables.description: Threshold for what is considered a large disk

variables.name: LargeDiskAbsolute
variables.description: What absolute value to monitor for large disks

variables.name: LargeDiskpercent
variables.description: what percentage to alert on for large disks

variables.name: MediumDiskAbsolute
variables.description: What absolute value to monitor for medium disks

variables.name: SmallDiskThreshold
variables.description: Threshold for what is considered a Small disk

variables.name: MediumDiskPercent
variables.description: what percentage to alert on for medium disks

variables.name: MediumdiskThreshold
variables.description: Threshold for what is considered a medium disk

variables.name: SmallDiskPercent
variables.description: what percentage to alert on for Small disks

variables.name: ExcludeUDF
variables.description: UDF to use for excluded disks.

7cd0c044-ae0a-49a1-97f8-8219fb82cd08.name: Software Detection Monitor [WIN]
7cd0c044-ae0a-49a1-97f8-8219fb82cd08.description: Sounds an alert if software identifying a known string is not discovered. Can be configured with a response Component to install the software in question. Searching using a full string (as opposed to a fragment) is highly recommended. Use equals sign in place of the space character.

variables.name: usrSearch
variables.description: Choose whether to search using a user-defined string (in usrString) or from a list of known applications.

variables.name: usrString
variables.description: The name of the software to look for. If usrSearch is not configured as 'defined in usrString', this is disregarded. Use equals sign to denote a space.

variables.name: usrMethod
variables.description: Determine monitor behaviour

3a4575f1-cfcc-417d-b377-b6fe6fe1eee1.name: LockBit Emulation [WIN]
3a4575f1-cfcc-417d-b377-b6fe6fe1eee1.description: Simulates a LockBit infection. The Component can be configured to run a cleanup routine immediately following the infection.

variables.name: usrRoutine
variables.description: Which procedure to run?

6a583e4b-7de6-4d18-a6b2-df9c03f32eca.name: CVE-2020-1472 Enforcement [WIN]
6a583e4b-7de6-4d18-a6b2-df9c03f32eca.description:
Deploys the Registry value required to enforce the fix for CVE-2020-1472. Deploying the patch alone is not enough to be 100% secure. Please see Microsoft's related advisory: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4557222/how-to-manage-the-changes-in-netlogon-secure-channel-connections-assoc
Required OS: Any Domain Controller
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-monitoring-driver-issues-monitoring-zerologon/

7e133177-6a35-42a6-84af-06530fb63a22.name: Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable (2015-2022) [WIN]
7e133177-6a35-42a6-84af-06530fb63a22.description: Downloads and installs the latest version of the Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable (2015-22) bundle for both architectures where applicable. Many applications require this runtime to be installed in order to function, including the Datto Windows Agent.

247a969e-e181-4d24-a9c5-264b56a12a71.name: TNA - Carbon Black (To install for Workstations and Servers)

f75b7054-60a9-44f1-90e7-6e55e062a7c9.name: VMware Tools [WIN]
f75b7054-60a9-44f1-90e7-6e55e062a7c9.description: Download and install the latest build of VMware Tools for your Windows VM's architecture. VMs may exhibit some screen refreshing during installation process. The device's WMI will be checked to ensure the software is only installed on GUESTS and not HOSTS; in special circumstances this check can be overridden by configuring a Site-/Global-level variable called 'usrVMTOverrideSITE' with the value 'true'.

225cc94d-e831-4591-b9a5-0fea0647348b.name: Shut-down Device [WIN]
225cc94d-e831-4591-b9a5-0fea0647348b.description: Shut-down device forcibly with variable time-out.

variables.name: timeout
variables.description: Set the time-out period before shutdown to X seconds

0953b5ed-4e00-4549-8746-66429fd70d96.name: Monitor Windows Firewall State [WIN]
0953b5ed-4e00-4549-8746-66429fd70d96.description:
Monitors the Windows Firewall state for both enablement and allow policies.
Alerts if the firewall is disabled and/or allowing all traffic. Required OS: Windows 8.1 and up.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-the-windows-firewall/

f91204c0-8410-45e2-8378-d9c658c56bc3.name: TNA - Deploy RMM Agent by AD GPO [WIN]
f91204c0-8410-45e2-8378-d9c658c56bc3.description:
Installs the Datto RMM Agent on next policy refresh for domain-controlled environments.

PLEASE REFER TO THE ONLINE DOCUMENTATION FOR FULL INFORMATION BEFORE USE: http://dat.to/rmmgpo

variables.name: RemoveGPO
variables.description: Control whether or not to use the component to remove the GPO and folder/contents; set to True to do this instead of setting up GPO deployment.

variables.name: RecreateCSV
variables.description: Control whether or not to recreate the CSV file required for OU targeting. Set both this variable and UseOUTargeting to True to do this, otherwise leave False

variables.name: ImmediateGPUpdate
variables.description: Control whether or not to run an immediate and silent Group Policy update across all your domain-joined computers once the GPO is imported and linked. True will do this. Set to False to wait for next automatic refresh

variables.name: UseOUTargeting
variables.description: Control targeting of GPO link(s) and/or destination sites. False will create a link at domain root and associate all computers to the DC site. Set to True to target by OU, and/or associate OUs to different sites, based on a pre-populated generated CSV file. First run will generate the file, subsequent run will deploy the Agents accordingly.

0708a260-ca1b-49f5-a7cf-fe7f876be7a3.name: Get ESXi ServiceTag into UDF [WIN]
0708a260-ca1b-49f5-a7cf-fe7f876be7a3.description:
This script will populate a UDF on the ESXi device in Datto RMM with the data that is in the ServiceTag field. 
When the Component is run, you must pick which UDF you want to populate (default 1) on the ESXi device. 

Pre-requisites for this script are :-
API Access to be Enabled and API credentials generated (See "Activate the API" here https://rmm.datto.com/help/en/Content/2SETUP/APIv2.htm) 

Two Global or Site-level variables created and populated with the aforementioned API access keys (See "Variables" here https://rmm.datto.com/help/en/Content/2SETUP/AccountSettings/AccountSettings.htm). The accAPISecret variable should be marked as "Masked" as it is highly sensitive.
  accAPIKey
  accAPISecret

variables.name: usrUDFNumber
variables.description: Enter a value between 1-30 for which UDF to populate on the ESXi host

cc957522-e129-4eb2-8798-397ecd4cc724.name: Get Windows Activation Status to User-Defined Field [WIN]
cc957522-e129-4eb2-8798-397ecd4cc724.description: Outputs the device's Windows Activation status to a User-Defined field.

variables.name: CUSTOMFLD
variables.description: Enter the number of the custom field you wish to populate (numeric value only).

8bd77fba-87fa-4865-805d-c80e82b71405.name: Hyper-V Host Location Tool [WIN]
8bd77fba-87fa-4865-805d-c80e82b71405.description: Run this against a Windows-based Hyper-V Guest to locate the hostname of the Hyper-V host hypervising it.

variables.name: usrUDFSetting
variables.description: UDF to write data to. Set to zero for no action or use numbers 1-30.

8d81f5f6-d7cb-4200-99f7-45b096da71c0.name: Approximate OS First-Install Date from Directories [WIN]
8d81f5f6-d7cb-4200-99f7-45b096da71c0.description: This script checks the creation dates of directories in Program Files to approximate a first-installation date for the Windows operating system where all other methods fail. This is intended to serve as a guideline. Can be configured to output install date as a date or just the year.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

variables.name: usrPreference
variables.description: Choose the format to output the installation date of the OS as approximated by the script as.

d57e137f-cea8-47cf-8515-8341e9d14869.name: Monitor and Generate WLAN Reports [WIN]
d57e137f-cea8-47cf-8515-8341e9d14869.description:
Monitors and generates WLAN reports for troubleshooting purposes. The WLAN report contains information about the computers current connections, certificates, disconnections, and other WLAN issues. Alerts if no report has been generated for 4 hours.

Recommended to not run more than hourly.

4abbe759-c0bb-4b3d-b907-0e4d701281d9.name: Monitor iSCSI Connection Status [WIN]
4abbe759-c0bb-4b3d-b907-0e4d701281d9.description:
Monitors the status of iSCSI connections. 

Supported OS: Server 2012R2 and up.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/370/

446a664a-f4a5-4e24-8a7f-32de77f8312c.name: Copy System Device Description into Datto RMM Description Field [WIN]
446a664a-f4a5-4e24-8a7f-32de77f8312c.description: Copy the system-level device description text into Datto RMM's description field for that device. Changes require an audit to take effect.

3af6e920-3730-4b38-a6a0-f7aedd58792f.name: Delete Named Admin Account (Active Directory) [WIN]
3af6e920-3730-4b38-a6a0-f7aedd58792f.description:
Deletes a named admin account user to prevent credentials reuse. For Active Directory servers.
Required OS: Server 2012R2 or higher.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-creating-named-accounts/

variables.name: AdminUserName
variables.description: Enter the username of the user you wish to delete

100c60b3-da48-48b6-83a5-2f5c813591f2.name: M365: Offboard User [WIN]
100c60b3-da48-48b6-83a5-2f5c813591f2.description:
Offboards a M365 user by executing the following tasks:

1.)Removes the user from all Azure AD groups
2.) Removes the user from all Teams
3.) Removes the user from all Distribution groups
4.) Hides the user from the GAL
5.) Converts the user to a shared mailbox
6.) Removes the licenses, and prints which license it removed for canceling.

Requires setup of M365 components first. Please see documentation.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-quickly-offboarding-a-m365-user/

variables.name: UserToOffboard
variables.description: UPN of user to offboard.

variables.name: ClientDomain
variables.description: The onmicrosoft domain for the cleint

81e7cc5e-66f2-4379-86b7-f8c6a4bd4bac.name: Microsoft Teams Automap [WIN]
81e7cc5e-66f2-4379-86b7-f8c6a4bd4bac.description:
Automaps all Microsoft Teams that the user has access to. Requires the AzAutomapper to be deployed.
Required OS: Windows 10.
Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-deploying-azure-functions/

variables.name: AutomapAPIKey
variables.description: The API key for the AzAutomapper function. This is also known in the azure portal as the "Function code"

variables.name: AutomapURL
variables.description: Your Azure function automapping URL

8d06689f-a83d-4abd-ab64-690d787afc09.name: Retrieve Windows Update History [WIN]
8d06689f-a83d-4abd-ab64-690d787afc09.description: This component retrieves the Windows Update history from a machine. Look at the STDout of this script to see the details.

a95a08a9-8b2e-4e1b-8a18-36a7cdfcc0ca.name: Shutdown/Reboot Device [WIN]
a95a08a9-8b2e-4e1b-8a18-36a7cdfcc0ca.description: Perform a shutdown or reboot operation on a Windows device with a configurable timeout period and message. BitLocker can optionally be suspended.

variables.name: usrAction
variables.description: Which power action to perform.

variables.name: usrMessage
variables.defaultVal: A shutdown operation has been mandated by your IT staff.
variables.description: Message for end users to see. Only shown if timeout is 60+ seconds.

variables.name: usrTimeout
variables.description: Amount of time to wait before performing action. Hours/Minutes/Seconds is set in usrTimeoutUnits.

variables.name: usrSuspendBitLocker
variables.description: Suspend BitLocker on the system drive, if encrypted

variables.name: usrTimeoutUnits
variables.description: Which unit to consider usrTimeout a quantity of.

31ab97c6-c164-4163-a954-53eed8b960a3.name: Splashtop Streamer Configuration Tool [WIN]
31ab97c6-c164-4163-a954-53eed8b960a3.description: Run this Component against an Endpoint to configure the manner in which Splashtop connections made to it are handled. Options are Idle timeout, audio muting, locking after a session and blocking endpoint input during sessions.

variables.name: usrAudio
variables.description: Configure whether audio played from the endpoint should also be played on the administrative device.

variables.name: usrRemoveSettings
variables.description: Remove all configurable Splashtop settings. Use this to return to a state before the Component was first run.

variables.name: usrTimeout
variables.description: Time in minutes past which an idle Splashtop connection will be killed. Use 0 to remove any timeout setting.

variables.name: usrLock
variables.description: Configure whether to lock the endpoint after a session on it has concluded.

variables.name: usrCapture
variables.description: Configure which capture method to use.

variables.name: usrBlockInput
variables.description: Configure whether to block keyboard and mouse input from the endpoint when it is connected to a Splashtop session.

variables.name: usrHardwareAcceleration
variables.description: Use hardware acceleration on remote devices when performing connections

variables.name: usrPersistentBanner
variables.description: Enable a persistent banner that displays on the end-user's screen during a Splashtop session.

variables.name: usrOptOut
variables.description: Opt-out of automatic uninstallation of Splashtop Streamer after thirty days' disuse.

7c77c3e8-4a17-4b4a-9304-27f174f26e2b.name: Telnet Client  - Enable [WIN]
7c77c3e8-4a17-4b4a-9304-27f174f26e2b.description:
Telnet Client  - Enable. 
By default Windows Vista, Server 2008 /2008 R2 and Windows 7 do not have the Telnet client available. This component will make the Telnet client available from the command-line.

f8cdc0d2-bdc2-47c4-bcd9-86f62d04ab6e.name: Toggle Windows News and Interest [WIN]
f8cdc0d2-bdc2-47c4-bcd9-86f62d04ab6e.description: Toggles whether Windows 10 systems that can display News and Interests will. Systems need a reboot before the change will take effect.

variables.name: usrEnable
variables.description: Display News and Interests

a60591ad-59ae-4aae-a3b9-0cdeed0b0b35.name: Toggle SYSTEM write access to Spool Drivers & Adjust Registry [WIN]
a60591ad-59ae-4aae-a3b9-0cdeed0b0b35.description: Toggle SYSTEM write capability to C:\Windows\System32\Spool\Drivers, a folder being actively targeted by PrintNightmare attacks. Can also adjust the Registry based on Microsoft's KB5005010.

variables.name: usrChoice
variables.description: Toggle SYSTEM write privileges

variables.name: usrRegistry
variables.description: Set Registry in accordance with https://preview.tinyurl.com/kb5005010

bce773fb-a784-4aaf-bb6a-cf2367da7741.name: Toggle Hiding Inactive System Tray Icons [WIN]
bce773fb-a784-4aaf-bb6a-cf2367da7741.description: Use this to toggle whether inactive system tray icons (including the Agent) are hidden or shown by Windows in the taskbar.

variables.name: usrHideInactive
variables.description: Hide inactive system tray icons in the taskbar?

bb229cb7-b667-4970-b2be-2e0ae38f99e5.name: Shrink VHD(x) of Hyper-V Virtual Machine [WIN]
bb229cb7-b667-4970-b2be-2e0ae38f99e5.description:
Automatically shuts down virtual machine, Runs Optimize-VHD to shrink disk, restarts VM when done. Required OS: Server 2012R2 or higher.
Related blog:https://www.cyberdrain.com/monitoring-with-powershell-chapter-2-hyper-v-replication-and-remediation/

variables.name: VMName
variables.description: Enter VMName. Wildcards allowed.

7234a376-6cb0-41a3-bcf1-3301a65edc80.name: Set System Time via Internet [WIN]
7234a376-6cb0-41a3-bcf1-3301a65edc80.description: Attempts to set the system's time to the correct local value using an NTP server. If the device is domain-joined, attempts will first be made to set the time using the domain controller.

variables.name: usrServer
variables.description: Place an NTP server here if you have a preference. NTP.org will be used if this is left blank.

f6f169ae-6b2c-4eb5-b58f-a593f00fee40.name: ShareX [WIN]
f6f169ae-6b2c-4eb5-b58f-a593f00fee40.description: This component will silently install ShareX on endpoints. ShareX is a open-source screenshot and screencast utility for Windows. Can be installed on Windows 7 SP1 and later

45ad467c-0f15-4f01-9e9f-86bef505d3c5.name: Write Data to UDF [WIN]
45ad467c-0f15-4f01-9e9f-86bef505d3c5.description: Writes arbitrary data to a user-specified UDF. Fields can be cleared by giving a space as UDF_Message's value.

variables.name: UDF_Number
variables.description: Which UDF? Enter a number 1 through 30

variables.name: UDF_Message
variables.defaultVal: What do you want the UDF to say?
variables.description: Input the value / info / message you want the UDF to display. 255 Characters MAX

3c5a11cd-b3dc-4b52-b0ab-2a32d82d8baa.name: Write Disk Type to UDF [WIN]
3c5a11cd-b3dc-4b52-b0ab-2a32d82d8baa.description: Writes the types of all detected disks to a UDF stating if they are SSDs or HDDs, including the Bus type. PowerShell 4.0+ required.

variables.name: UDFNumber
variables.description: Number of UDF where information should be stored.

0ca2a0ca-c439-4de6-b7df-b7313844b273.name: Security Endpoint - Carbon Black
0ca2a0ca-c439-4de6-b7df-b7313844b273.description: This will check if Carbon Black is installed and output to UDF

1c24353c-4026-4e9a-a71f-563161afe2b6.name: Liongard Agent [WIN]
1c24353c-4026-4e9a-a71f-563161afe2b6.description: Installs the Liongard Agent (old name 'Roar'). Installation on an Active Directory server is recommended but not mandatory.

variables.name: usrRoarAccessSecret
variables.description: Your Roar API secret. This must be generated from the Roar interface (User menu > Account Settings > Access Tokens)

variables.name: usrRoarURL
variables.description: The Roar URL for your instance up to the TLD; e.g., us1.app.liongard.com.

variables.name: usrRoarSVCUsername
variables.description: User account to run Roar under. Leave blank if usrADServer is false or to run as System. Will be created if new. Do not include domain.

variables.name: usrRoarAccessKey
variables.description: Your Roar API access key. This must be generated from the Roar interface (User menu > Account Settings > Access Tokens)

variables.name: usrRoarSVCPassword
variables.description: State the password to Run As - or create with - here. Leave blank if the agent should run in system context. Mandatory if usrADServer is true.

variables.name: usrGroup
variables.description: If not set, the script will lookup either the local admin or the domain admin group. If you want to join the service user to a different group, enter the group name.

variables.name: usrADServer
variables.description: If the device is an AD joined Server, set to TRUE. Credentials entered in usrLiongardSVCUsername and usrLiongardSVCPassword are handled as a domain account. If set to FALSE, the credentials are handled as local accounts.

variables.name: usrAgentType
variables.description: Which Agent Type to use?

variables.name: usrLiongardAccessKey
variables.description: Your Liongard API access key. This must be generated from the Liongard interface (User menu > Account Settings > Access Tokens)

variables.name: usrLiongardAccessSecret
variables.description: Your Liongard API secret. This must be generated from the Liongard interface (User menu > Account Settings > Access Tokens)

variables.name: usrLiongardURL
variables.description: The Liongard URL for your instance up to the TLD; e.g., us1.app.liongard.com.

variables.name: usrLiongardSVCUsername
variables.description: User account to run Liongard under. Leave blank if usrADServer is false or to run as System. Will be created if new. Do not include domain.

variables.name: usrLiongardSVCPassword
variables.description: State the password to Run As - or create with - here. Leave blank if the agent should run in system context. Mandatory if usrADServer is true.

637c4b87-f67a-4f4b-8c83-752a362b2bc6.name: Splashtop Streamer Pre-Installer [WIN]
637c4b87-f67a-4f4b-8c83-752a362b2bc6.description: This component will install the Splashtop Remote Control Streamer on a Windows Endpoint. By running this component you can make sure the streamer is present at the time you want to connect to the device; it also supports registration of the Splashtop-for-Business service either on the variable-level or on the Site- or Account-level using a variable named usrSplashtopKeySITE. This Component enables the Persistent Banner option by default.

581e0ef1-104e-42b1-9835-4924782fe15c.name: Install & Configure SysMon [WIN]
581e0ef1-104e-42b1-9835-4924782fe15c.description:
Installs and configures sysmon using the advised community settings(https://github.com/olafhartong/sysmon-modular) 

System Monitor (Sysmon) is a Windows system service and device driver that, once installed on a system, remains resident across system reboots to monitor and log system activity to the Windows event log. It provides detailed information about process creations, network connections, and changes to file creation time. By collecting the events it generates using Windows Event Collection or SIEM agents and subsequently analyzing them, you can identify malicious or anomalous activity and understand how intruders and malware operate on your network.

0afc8947-1465-41b5-bbc1-158a97deeca3.name: Hanrob OffBoard user from AD
0afc8947-1465-41b5-bbc1-158a97deeca3.description:
To offboard user from AD
This script will do the following
1.) Search for the user with Display name provided
2.) will create new group "disabled users" so that it can be set as primary. 
3.) Script will end if there are multiple users with same Display Name
4.) Add user to "disabled Users" group and set it as primary. Disable the user
5.) Remove the user from AD groups and run the AD sync.
6.) Hide the user from Exchange address list if requested.
7.) Move the disabled user to Disabled Users Ou.

variables.name: DisplayName
variables.description: Enter the Display Name of the user to be Offboarded.

variables.name: hide
variables.description: Enter Yes if you want to hide. All other values will not hide it.

77669e04-9198-42dc-af4f-7390d4168dd6.name: Hanrob - M365: Offboard User [WIN]
77669e04-9198-42dc-af4f-7390d4168dd6.description:
Offboards a M365 user by executing the following tasks:

1.) Removes the user from all Azure AD groups (Static)
2.) Removes the user from all Teams
3.) Removes the user's full mailbox access and send as access from any other mailbox.
4.) Removes the user from all Distribution groups
5.) Converts the user to a shared mailbox
6.) Removes the licenses, and prints which license it removed for canceling.
7.) Sets the sign in status to blocked for the user.

Requires setup of M365 components first. Please see documentation.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-quickly-offboarding-a-m365-user/

variables.description: UPN of user to offboard.

f2cd282f-0b28-48be-ad5d-796b0011fef1.description:
Offboards a M365 user by executing the following tasks:

1.) Removes the user from all Azure AD groups (Static)
2.) Removes the user from two dynamic groups "All Users"
3.) Removes the user from all Teams
4.) Removes the user's full mailbox access and send as access from any other mailbox.
5.) Removes the user from all Distribution groups
6.) Hides the user from the GAL
7.) Converts the user to a shared mailbox
8.) Removes the licenses, and prints which license it removed for canceling.
9.) Updates the Title of the user to reflect "- Ex Employee"
10.) Sets the sign in status to blocked for the user.

Requires setup of M365 components first. Please see documentation.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-quickly-offboarding-a-m365-user/

c179a7c1-3716-4c8f-9795-7e20dd85e1e8.description:
Install or Uninstall Malwarebytes Endpoint Agent on Windows devices with this component - Be aware there are reports that this installer may reboot devices.

When scheduling a job using this component, select install, uninstall or repair in the 'run_type' Variables drop-down. 

NOTE: The OneView Site's Account Token must be defined at Site- or Account-level as 'MWB_AccountToken' in order to properly deploy the Endpoint Agent and report to the correct Site. Specify an anti-tamper password by defining the MWB_Tamper_password Site variable. For more information on endpoint system requirements, refer to the Malwarebytes Support Documentation: https://support.malwarebytes.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038522274-System-requirements-for-Malwarebytes-Nebula-

variables.name: MWB_runtype
variables.description: Specifiy if endpoint agent will be installed or uninstalled. Tamper-protection MUST BE OFF for uninstall. Delete Corrupted Endpoint option will fully delete Malwarebytes EPA from Device and restart it.

fb6d3fb3-7c5e-457f-902d-990ca11c08d1.name: Techants - M365: Offboard User [WIN]
fb6d3fb3-7c5e-457f-902d-990ca11c08d1.description:
Offboards a M365 user by executing the following tasks:

1.) Removes the user from all Azure AD groups (Static)
2.) Removes the user from all Teams
3.) Removes the user's full mailbox access and send as access from any other mailbox.
4.) Removes the user from all Distribution groups
5.) Converts the user to a shared mailbox
6.) Removes the licenses, and prints which license it removed for canceling.
7.) Sets the sign in status to blocked for the user.

Requires setup of M365 components first. Please see documentation.

Related blog: https://www.cyberdrain.com/automating-with-powershell-quickly-offboarding-a-m365-user/

7aa05710-1dec-471a-a18c-ed3b37a8e785.name: Tharawal Deploy RMM Agent by AD GPO
7aa05710-1dec-471a-a18c-ed3b37a8e785.description:
Installs the Datto RMM Agent on next policy refresh for domain-controlled environments.

PLEASE REFER TO THE ONLINE DOCUMENTATION FOR FULL INFORMATION BEFORE USE: http://dat.to/rmmgpo

variables.name: RemoveGPO
variables.description: Control whether or not to use the component to remove the GPO and folder/contents; set to True to do this instead of setting up GPO deployment.

variables.name: ImmediateGPUpdate
variables.description: Control whether or not to run an immediate and silent Group Policy update across all your domain-joined computers once the GPO is imported and linked. True will do this. Set to False to wait for next automatic refresh

variables.name: UseOUTargeting
variables.description: Control targeting of GPO link(s) and/or destination sites. False will create a link at domain root and associate all computers to the DC site. Set to True to target by OU, and/or associate OUs to different sites, based on a pre-populated generated CSV file. First run will generate the file, subsequent run will deploy the Agents accordingly.

variables.name: RecreateCSV
variables.description: Control whether or not to recreate the CSV file required for OU targeting. Set both this variable and UseOUTargeting to True to do this, otherwise leave False

a87eb27f-a2b0-472b-95e5-a1ede53cfe49.description: Resets a device using Windows Reset (Windows 10 or higher required). Will delete all data off the device. Has two options: quick reset and full wipe. The full wipe can brick the device if interrupted.

f7a05917-2ab7-4374-8615-1fadf4f5dc43.name: WinHTTP Proxy (Add) [WIN]
f7a05917-2ab7-4374-8615-1fadf4f5dc43.description: This component will add a system level proxy setting on the target device. The Microsoft Windows Update client requires Microsoft Windows HTTP Services (WinHTTP) to scan for available updates. Microsoft Windows HTTP Services run independently of Microsoft Internet Explorer and cannot auto detect proxy settings that may have been set from within a user account. Please see http://support.microsoft.com/kb/900935 for further details.

variables.name: WUAProxyPortNumber
variables.description: Proxy port to use in the format: 8080

variables.name: WUAProxyServerName
variables.description: Proxy server name to use in the format of: my.proxy.net

c2b4dca7-464c-4b17-9849-c44d073ade87.name: WinHTTP Proxy (Remove) [WIN]
c2b4dca7-464c-4b17-9849-c44d073ade87.description: This component will remove a system level proxy setting on the target device. The Microsoft Windows Update client requires Microsoft Windows HTTP Services (WinHTTP) to scan for available updates. Microsoft Windows HTTP Services run independently of Microsoft Internet Explorer and cannot auto detect proxy settings that may have been set from within a user account. Please see http://support.microsoft.com/kb/900935 for further details.

8af529fb-bb74-4894-af7e-de4ce3ae273e.name: Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8 [WIN]
8af529fb-bb74-4894-af7e-de4ce3ae273e.description: Use this Component to install version 4.8 of the .NET Framework on devices capable of running it. The installation may take upwards of 20 minutes.

faed5c69-0013-4bfc-aa1f-fd11766754fc.name: Internet Connection Checkup [WIN]
faed5c69-0013-4bfc-aa1f-fd11766754fc.description: Provides a report listing various connection statistics which can aid in the troubleshooting of complaints of poor internet connectivity. Also performs an internal speed test.

89d7bddb-77fe-4c60-90e1-f82b19cb6fb9.name: Audit Wi-Fi Connection [MAC]
89d7bddb-77fe-4c60-90e1-f82b19cb6fb9.description: Performs a simple audit of the wireless network/s the device is connected to (if any) and the strength of the connection.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

f1ba401d-bcb2-4bba-9bba-61d60df46355.name: Toggle Script-Block Logging [WIN]
f1ba401d-bcb2-4bba-9bba-61d60df46355.description: Enables or disables script-block logging, a feature which can be leveraged by the 'PowerShell Events' Best-Practice Policy to check for harmful PowerShell commands. The status of a device's script-block logging can be written to a UDF.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

variables.name: usrAction
variables.description: Enable or disable script-block logging?

5db4f7ff-9a97-43a6-bd55-9bdf76af9ef8.name: Chocolatey v2 [WIN]
5db4f7ff-9a97-43a6-bd55-9bdf76af9ef8.description: Installs and controls the Chocolatey community-based package manager. Can be used to install or update packages by their Chocolatey ID (accessible via https://community.chocolatey.org/packages). A maximum waiting period can be configured before downloads start to help evade bandwidth throttling.

variables.name: usrWaitThreshold
variables.description: Maximum amount of minutes to wait before starting the script. Randomised per-endpoint. Blank to start immediately.

variables.name: usrAppsToInstall
variables.description: Give IDs of apps to install with Chocolatey (separate entries with a space), taken from https://community.chocolatey.org/packages, or leave blank.

variables.name: usrAppsToUpdate
variables.description: Give IDs of Chocolatey-managed apps to update, leave blank, or set to 'ALL' to update all managed apps with Chocolatey. Separate entries with a space.

2f8c525a-263c-49be-9fd1-4c0dd2904a88.name: CVE-2023-36884 (Storm-0978) Protection [WIN]
2f8c525a-263c-49be-9fd1-4c0dd2904a88.description: Patches Microsoft Office 365 apps (and WordPad) against zero-day vulnerabilities outlined in CVE-2023-36884 using the 'block cross-protocol file navigation' method. This fix can be reverted if it causes issues. More information: https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2023-36884

variables.name: usrAction
variables.description: The protection can be reverted if it causes issues or becomes unnecessary.

variables.name: usrForceClose
variables.description: Forcibly close applications to engage protection? This will likely lead to work being lost.

e22f2d07-2e4b-48f0-b881-b0f45e9d6e15.name: Get Default Browser [MAC]
e22f2d07-2e4b-48f0-b881-b0f45e9d6e15.description: Audits the default browser for all users and optionally puts this information into a UDF.

variables.name: usrUDF
variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

e1d17d8e-eb47-4e58-9b74-a74dafad6f85.name: Remove Desktop Shortcuts [WIN]
e1d17d8e-eb47-4e58-9b74-a74dafad6f85.description: Removes shortcuts from all users' desktops. Can be configured to remove shortcuts commonly added by Software Management, all discovered shortcuts, or a custom user-defined list. Does not affect URL shortcuts.

variables.name: usrShortcuts
variables.description: Remove which list of shortcuts?

variables.name: usrCustomShortcuts
variables.description: Comma-separated list of shortcut names to seek and remove. Only used for 'Custom' option.

7ae1bb9e-6f99-48b3-8da4-dfc698f763b7.name: Bitdefender GravityZone Endpoint Management [Datto Companion APP]
7ae1bb9e-6f99-48b3-8da4-dfc698f763b7.description: Use this component to deploy and schedule auto-deployment jobs for the Bitdefender security agent. The Bitdefender agent runs the installation files through Setup Downloader (small-sized file). Component installation requires an MSP trial or monthly subscription and a Partner account to access Bitdefender GravityZone, a unified management console for advanced endpoint protection. The 'Bitdefender GravityZone Endpoint Management' Component is also used to configure response actions for monitoring policies for the new Datto Companion APP service (https://datto.rmm.bitdefender.com/login). Through the available response actions, you can deploy or update the security agent. For more information, refer to the Bitdefender Datto Companion APP User Guide (https://www.bitdefender.com/business/support/en/77211-321685-datto-rmm.html).

variables.name: Action
variables.description: Select an action that the device management component needs to perform.

e61ae079-c511-43b3-be06-4e6645629c06.name: Install IT Glue Browser Extension [WIN]
e61ae079-c511-43b3-be06-4e6645629c06.description: Installs the IT Glue Browser Extension for Google Chrome, Edge (Chromium) and Mozilla Firefox. May work for Chromium derivatives. Administrators are encouraged to couple deployment of this Component with a user advisory detailing how to enable it post-installation.

41d24180-b0d9-4ea7-8d44-72400f606308.name: Microsoft Outlook CVE-2023-23397 [WIN]
41d24180-b0d9-4ea7-8d44-72400f606308.description: Patches Microsoft Outlook 2013, 2016, 2019 and 365 against CVE-2023-23397 using either the Microsoft Click2Run client or Microsoft Patches for 2013 and 2016. Made with help from the Datto Community.

variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

f023e2d1-861d-4560-a385-fde0e304a71c.description: Produce a list of all local users on the device and whether their accounts are flagged as Administrators, Enabled, and Active on the system.

variables.description: Place summary data into which UDF (if any)?

variables.description: Exit with a warning if enabled local Administrator accounts are present on the system.

211fab32-8139-4924-9020-6a5e5a8200dd.description: Uses the Windows 11 Installation Assistant to upgrade or update a device running Windows 10 (2004 or newer) or 11 (any) to the latest version of Windows 11. OS Language should be preserved. Supports Enterprise; untested on Education or Pro-for-Workstations devices.

variables.name: usrOverrideChecks
variables.description: Consent to overriding best-practice checks worked into the script to avoid unnecessary bandwidth wastage or system damage. If this is set to TRUE, Support will be unable to assist with issues that arise.
